text:
mild nonspecific inflammatory infiltrate in the lamina propria
-	Ulceration/erosion of the mucosa
-	Signs of chronicity: architectural distortion of the crypts, Paneth cell metaplasia in the colon, goblet cell depletion
Table 3: Paris classification for pediatric Crohn's disease (4)
Age at diagnosis
A1a:	0-10 years
A1b:	10-17 years
A2:	17-40 years
A3:	> 40 years
Location (L = "location")
L1:	distal third of the ileum ± limited cecal involvement
L2:	colon only
L3:	ileocolonic
L4a:	upper gastrointestinal involvement proximal to the ligament of Treitz
L4b:	jejunal and/or proximal two-thirds of the ileum
Behavior (B = "behavior")
B1:	inflammatory
B2:	stricturing
B3:	fistulizing
B2B3:	both stricturing and fistulizing, either at the same time or at different times
p:	perianal disease
Growth (G = "growth")
G0:	no growth delay
G1:	growth delay
